title: Softmax-recip subtile pipeline schedule for Llama7B attention
hypothesis: After measured SRAM rebalance, measured HBM service, and HBM-closed on-chip schedule closure, the remaining tile-attention bottleneck can be reduced by streaming QK, softmax, and value work across legal subtiles within the measured local SRAM capacity.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does sub-tile QK/softmax/V pipelining improve the softmax-recip HBM-closed Llama7B attention frontier without exceeding measured local SRAM capacity?
include: Sweep subtile count, subtile buffer count, prefetch distance, normalization strategy, compute sharing mode, softmax latency per subtile, and online rescale penalty.
Require the stream buffer footprint to fit within the measured local capacity per cluster.
Report latency, speedup versus the HBM-closed source, dominant resource, selected subtile schedule, residual HBM exposure, and local capacity margin.
exclude: Do not change HBM controller parameters or SRAM macro packing in this item.
Do not change precision, KV sharing, or numerical quality assumptions.
Do not add new full NoC RTL or a new compute primitive in this schedule-only item.
Do not treat the dual-MAC schedule as free of area impact until the compute partition model is revisited.
follow_on_broad_sweep: If the schedule improves latency, revisit compute-area accounting for dual-MAC and split-MAC modes.
If the pipeline remains dominant, explore physical compute-path pipelining and additional attention datapath partitioning.
If HBM exposure or local SRAM becomes dominant, move to a more detailed memory-controller and local-buffer schedule model.

expected_benefit: Reduces the tile-attention critical path after memory-service closure.
Tests whether the measured local SRAM capacity can support streaming subtile buffers.
Identifies whether the next bottleneck is pipeline compute, residual memory service, or HBM exposure.
risks: Dual-MAC mode currently inherits the source compute area and should be revisited for physical area consistency.
Online correction and softmax rescale behavior are schedule-mode assumptions rather than full numerical validation.
The model remains analytic and does not yet instantiate a full pipelined RTL datapath.

expected_result.reason: Evaluate whether sub-tile QK/softmax/V pipelining reduces the tile_attention bottleneck after softmax-recip measured-HBM and HBM-closed on-chip service closure.
